title: artocarpus integer var. silvestris
intro: Artocarpus integer var. silvestris, also known as the wild jackfruit, is a tropical plant that belongs to the family Moraceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The wild jackfruit has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and regulate digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds, leaves, and bark can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed orally or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
